docs.title: OmniDeploy
docs.text:
Universal deployment tool for container applications.

Deploy to any cloud provider using any Infrastructure as Code tool.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
OmniDeploy separates where you deploy (targets) from how you provision (backends), giving you flexibility to choose the best combination for your needs.

                    Backend (HOW to provision)
                    ┌─────────┬─────────┬───────────┐
                    │ Pulumi  │   CDK   │ Terraform │
        ┌───────────┼─────────┼─────────┼───────────┤
        │ LightSail │    ✓    │    ◐    │     ◐     │
Target  │ ECS       │    ◐    │    ◐    │     ◐     │
(WHERE) │ AgentCore │    ◐    │    ◐    │     ◐     │
        │ Kubernetes│    ◐    │    -    │     ◐     │
        │ DigitalOcean│  ◐    │    -    │     ◐     │
        └───────────┴─────────┴─────────┴───────────┘

✓ = Available  ◐ = Planned  - = Not applicable

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Multi-Target: Deploy to AWS LightSail, ECS, Kubernetes, DigitalOcean, and more
  • Multi-Backend: Use Pulumi, AWS CDK, or Terraform as your IaC tool
  • Runtime Adapters: Auto-detect OmniAgent, AgentKit, or generic container configs
  • Simple CLI: One command to deploy, preview, or destroy
  • CI/CD Ready: Built-in support for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest

# Deploy to AWS LightSail using Pulumi
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --target lightsail --backend pulumi

# Preview changes
omnideploy preview --config deploy.yaml

# Destroy
omnideploy destroy --stack my-app

docs.title: Example Configuration
docs.text:
name: my-api
region: us-east-1

container:
  image: nginx:latest
  ports:
    - container_port: 80
      protocol: HTTP

service:
  replicas: 1
  public: true

resources:
  size: micro

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
OmniDeploy uses a pluggable architecture with three main components:

  1. Targets - Define where to deploy (LightSail, ECS, Kubernetes)
  2. Backends - Define how to provision (Pulumi, CDK, Terraform)
  3. Runtime Adapters - Translate app configs to deployment configs
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 Your Application                     │
│         (OmniAgent, AgentKit, Container)            │
└─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                          │
                          ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Runtime Adapter                         │
│    Converts app config → deployment config          │
└─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                          │
                          ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Deployment Config                       │
│     (containers, ports, resources, health)          │
└─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                          │
            ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
            ▼             ▼             ▼
      ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐
      │ LightSail│  │   ECS    │  │   K8s    │
      │  Target  │  │  Target  │  │  Target  │
      └────┬─────┘  └────┬─────┘  └────┬─────┘
           │             │             │
           └─────────────┼─────────────┘
                         ▼
            ┌─────────────────────────┐
            │      IaC Backend        │
            │  (Pulumi, CDK, TF)      │
            └─────────────────────────┘

docs.title: IaC Backends
docs.text: Backends define how cloud resources are provisioned.

docs.title: Available Backends
docs.text: Backend Status Description State Storage Pulumi ✓ Available Pulumi Automation API Local or cloud CDK ◐ Planned AWS Cloud Development Kit CloudFormation Terraform ◐ Planned Terraform HCL generation Local or remote

docs.title: Pulumi
docs.text:
Best for:

  • Go developers (native Go SDK)
  • Teams already using Pulumi
  • Flexibility in state storage

State options: Local filesystem, S3, Pulumi Cloud

docs.title: AWS CDK
docs.text:
Best for:

  • AWS-only deployments
  • Teams familiar with CloudFormation
  • Tight AWS integration

State: Managed by CloudFormation

docs.title: Terraform
docs.text:
Best for:

  • Teams with Terraform expertise
  • Multi-cloud deployments
  • Existing Terraform workflows

State options: Local, S3, Terraform Cloud

docs.title: Backend Comparison
docs.text: Feature Pulumi CDK Terraform Language Go Go/TS/Py HCL State management Flexible CloudFormation Flexible Preview/Plan ✓ ✓ ✓ Drift detection ✓ Limited ✓ Multi-cloud ✓ AWS only ✓ Learning curve Medium Medium Medium

docs.title: Using Backends
docs.text:
Specify backend with --backend flag:

# Pulumi (default)
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --backend pulumi

# CDK
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --backend cdk

# Terraform
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --backend terraform

docs.title: State Management
docs.text: Each backend handles state differently:

docs.title: Pulumi
docs.text:
Default: ~/.omnideploy/pulumi/

Configure remote:

export PULUMI_BACKEND_URL=s3://my-bucket/pulumi-state

docs.title: CDK
docs.text: State managed by AWS CloudFormation. No local state files.

docs.title: Terraform
docs.text:
Default: ~/.omnideploy/terraform/

Configure remote:

export TF_BACKEND_TYPE=s3
export TF_BACKEND_BUCKET=my-bucket
export TF_BACKEND_KEY=omnideploy/terraform.tfstate

docs.title: Switching Backends
docs.text:
Backends are independent. Switching backends creates new resources (doesn't migrate).

To migrate:

  1. Deploy with new backend
  2. Verify deployment works
  3. Destroy old backend's resources
# Deploy with new backend
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --backend terraform --stack my-app-tf

# Test
curl https://new-url/health

# Destroy old
omnideploy destroy --stack my-app --backend pulumi

docs.title: List Available Backends
docs.text:
omnideploy backends

Output:

Available backends:
  pulumi       Pulumi - Infrastructure as Code using Go

docs.title: Pulumi Backend
docs.text: Infrastructure as Code using Pulumi's Automation API.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Pulumi backend uses Pulumi's Go SDK and Automation API to:

  • Create and manage cloud resources
  • Track state changes
  • Preview deployments
  • Handle rollbacks

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• AWS credentials configured
  • (Optional) Pulumi CLI for advanced operations

docs.title: Local State (Default)
docs.text:
State is stored at ~/.omnideploy/pulumi/.

ls ~/.omnideploy/pulumi/
# Pulumi.yaml, Pulumi.my-app.yaml, etc.

docs.title: AWS S3 Backend
docs.text:
For team collaboration:

export PULUMI_BACKEND_URL=s3://my-bucket/omnideploy-state

# Then deploy
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: Pulumi Cloud
docs.text:
For managed state:

# Install Pulumi CLI
brew install pulumi

# Login to Pulumi Cloud
pulumi login

# Deploy (uses Pulumi Cloud for state)
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: Deploy
docs.text: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --backend pulumi

docs.title: Preview
docs.text: omnideploy preview --config deploy.yaml --backend pulumi

docs.title: Destroy
docs.text: omnideploy destroy --stack my-app --backend pulumi

docs.title: Refresh State
docs.text:
Sync state with actual cloud resources:

omnideploy refresh --stack my-app

docs.title: Advanced Operations
docs.text: For operations not available in omnideploy, use Pulumi CLI:

docs.title: View Stack State
docs.text:
cd ~/.omnideploy/pulumi
pulumi stack select my-app
pulumi stack

docs.title: Export State
docs.text: pulumi stack export > state.json

docs.title: Import Resources
docs.text: pulumi import aws:lightsail/containerService:ContainerService my-app my-app

docs.title: View History
docs.text: pulumi history

docs.title: Stack Configuration
docs.text:
Set stack-specific config:

cd ~/.omnideploy/pulumi
pulumi stack select my-app
pulumi config set aws:region us-west-2

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text:
Store secrets securely:

pulumi config set --secret db_password mypassword

Access in code:

password := cfg.RequireSecret("db_password")

docs.title: State Lock
docs.text:
If deployment fails mid-way:

cd ~/.omnideploy/pulumi
pulumi cancel

docs.title: Corrupt State
docs.text:
Export, fix, and import:

pulumi stack export > state.json
# Edit state.json
pulumi stack import < state.json

docs.title: Resource Drift
docs.text:
Refresh to detect drift:

omnideploy refresh --stack my-app

Then re-deploy to fix:

omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack my-app

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text:
- name: Setup Pulumi
  uses: pulumi/actions@v5

- name: Deploy
  env:
    PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
  run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

docs.title: With S3 Backend
docs.text:
- name: Deploy
  env:
    PULUMI_BACKEND_URL: s3://${{ secrets.STATE_BUCKET }}/omnideploy
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
  run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Terraform provides:

  • HCL configuration generation
  • Multi-cloud support
  • Terraform Cloud integration
  • Extensive provider ecosystem
  • State management flexibility

docs.title: Planned Features
docs.text:
• HCL file generation
  • Terraform Cloud integration

  • Module generation

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
Choose Terraform when:

  • Team has Terraform expertise
  • Multi-cloud deployments
  • Existing Terraform infrastructure
  • Want HCL files for review

docs.title: Configuration Preview
docs.text:
OmniDeploy will generate Terraform HCL:

# Generated by omnideploy

resource "aws_lightsail_container_service" "my_app" {
  name        = "my-app"
  power       = "micro"
  scale       = 1
  is_disabled = false
}

resource "aws_lightsail_container_service_deployment_version" "my_app" {
  service_name = aws_lightsail_container_service.my_app.name

  container {
    container_name = "my-app"
    image          = "nginx:latest"

    ports = {
      80 = "HTTP"
    }
  }

  public_endpoint {
    container_name = "my-app"
    container_port = 80

    health_check {
      path = "/health"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Usage Preview
docs.text:
# Generate Terraform files
omnideploy generate --config deploy.yaml --backend terraform --output ./terraform/

# Review
cd terraform
terraform plan

# Apply
terraform apply

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Configure OmniDeploy and your deployments using environment variables.

docs.title: OmniDeploy Configuration
docs.text: Variable Description Default OMNIDEPLOY_WORK_DIR Working directory for state ~/.omnideploy

docs.title: AWS
docs.text: Variable Description AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID AWS access key ID AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY AWS secret access key AWS_SESSION_TOKEN AWS session token (for temporary credentials) AWS_REGION Default AWS region AWS_PROFILE AWS CLI profile name

docs.title: DigitalOcean
docs.text: Variable Description DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN DigitalOcean API token

docs.title: Pulumi
docs.text: Variable Description PULUMI_BACKEND_URL Remote state URL (s3://, gs://, azblob://) PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN Pulumi Cloud access token PULUMI_CONFIG_PASSPHRASE Passphrase for local secrets encryption

docs.title: Terraform
docs.text: Variable Description TF_VAR_* Terraform variable values TF_BACKEND_* Terraform backend configuration

docs.title: Variable Expansion
docs.text:
Environment variables can be referenced in config files:

environment:
  # Direct reference
  API_KEY: ${API_KEY}

  # With default value
  LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-info}

  # Nested reference
  DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL}

docs.title: Expansion Syntax
docs.text: Syntax Description ${VAR} Required variable (error if missing) ${VAR:-default} Default if variable is unset or empty ${VAR-default} Default only if variable is unset $$ Literal dollar sign

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# deploy.yaml
name: my-app

environment:
  # Required - deployment fails if missing
  DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL}

  # Optional with defaults
  LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
  PORT: ${PORT:-8080}

  # Literal dollar sign
  PRICE: $$100

Deploy with:

DATABASE_URL=postgres://... LOG_LEVEL=debug omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: Shell Export
docs.text:
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA...
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
export DATABASE_URL=postgres://...

omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: Inline
docs.text: DATABASE_URL=postgres://... omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: dotenv File
docs.text:
Create .env:

AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA...
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
DATABASE_URL=postgres://...

Load and deploy:

export $(cat .env | xargs) && omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: direnv
docs.text:
Create .envrc:

export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA...
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
export DATABASE_URL=postgres://...

Allow and use:

direnv allow
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Never commit secrets: Add .env to .gitignore
  2. Use secret managers: AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault
  3. Rotate credentials: Regularly rotate API keys and passwords
  4. Least privilege: Use IAM roles with minimal permissions
  5. Audit access: Enable CloudTrail for AWS API logging

docs.title: Configuration Schema
docs.text: Complete reference for OmniDeploy configuration files.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: OmniDeploy uses YAML or JSON configuration files. The schema is designed to be cloud-agnostic while supporting target-specific features.

docs.title: Full Schema
docs.text:
# =============================================================================
# IDENTITY
# =============================================================================

# Required: Unique name for this deployment
name: my-app

# Optional: Version tag (used for image tagging and tracking)
version: "1.0.0"

# Optional: Cloud region (default: us-east-1)
region: us-east-1

# =============================================================================
# CONTAINER
# =============================================================================

container:
  # Required: Container image URL
  image: nginx:latest

  # Optional: Override container entrypoint
  command:
    - /bin/sh
    - -c

  # Optional: Arguments to entrypoint
  args:
    - "nginx -g 'daemon off;'"

  # Optional: Working directory inside container
  working_dir: /app

  # Required: At least one port
  ports:
    - container_port: 80      # Required: Port number (1-65535)
      protocol: HTTP          # HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, UDP (default: TCP)
      name: http              # Optional: Port name

  # Optional: Health check configuration
  health_check:
    path: /health             # HTTP path to check
    port: 80                  # Port to check (default: first port)
    interval: 30s             # Time between checks (default: 30s)
    timeout: 5s               # Timeout per check (default: 5s)
    healthy_threshold: 2      # Consecutive successes (default: 2)
    unhealthy_threshold: 3    # Consecutive failures (default: 3)

# =============================================================================
# SERVICE
# =============================================================================

service:
  # Optional: Number of container instances (default: 1)
  replicas: 1

  # Optional: Make service publicly accessible (default: false)
  public: true

  # Optional: Custom domain names
  domains:
    - api.example.com
    - www.example.com

  # Optional: TLS/HTTPS configuration
  tls:
    enabled: true
    certificate_arn: arn:aws:acm:...  # AWS-specific
    auto_cert: false                   # Auto-provision certificate

# =============================================================================
# RESOURCES
# =============================================================================

resources:
  # Optional: Preset size (target-specific)
  # LightSail: nano, micro, small, medium, large, xlarge
  # ECS: Defined by cpu/memory below
  size: micro

  # Optional: CPU in millicores (e.g., 256 = 0.25 vCPU)
  cpu: 256

  # Optional: Memory in MB
  memory: 512

# =============================================================================
# ENVIRONMENT
# =============================================================================

# Optional: Environment variables (key-value pairs)
environment:
  LOG_LEVEL: info
  DATABASE_URL: postgres://...

  # Environment variable expansion
  API_KEY: ${API_KEY}           # From shell environment
  HOME_DIR: ${HOME:-/root}      # With default value

# =============================================================================
# SECRETS
# =============================================================================

# Optional: Secret references (target-specific)
secrets:
  - name: DATABASE_PASSWORD     # Env var name in container
    source: ssm:/my-app/db-pass # Source (ssm:, secretsmanager:, vault:)

  - name: API_KEY
    source: secretsmanager:my-app/api-key

# =============================================================================
# TAGS
# =============================================================================

# Optional: Resource tags (for cost tracking, organization)
tags:
  environment: production
  team: platform
  cost-center: engineering
  managed-by: omnideploy

docs.title: Top-Level Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Default Description name string Yes - Deployment name (alphanumeric, hyphens) version string No - Version string for tracking region string No us-east-1 Cloud region container object Yes - Container configuration service object No - Service configuration resources object No - Resource allocation environment map No - Environment variables secrets array No - Secret references tags map No - Resource tags

docs.title: Container Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Default Description image string Yes - Container image URL command array No - Entrypoint override args array No - Command arguments working_dir string No - Working directory ports array Yes - Port mappings (at least one) health_check object No - Health check config

docs.title: Port Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Default Description container_port int Yes - Port number (1-65535) protocol string No TCP HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, UDP name string No - Port name for reference

docs.title: Health Check Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Default Description path string No - HTTP path (e.g., /health) port int No First port Port to check interval duration No 30s Time between checks timeout duration No 5s Check timeout healthy_threshold int No 2 Consecutive successes unhealthy_threshold int No 3 Consecutive failures

docs.title: Service Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Default Description replicas int No 1 Number of instances public bool No false Public accessibility domains array No - Custom domain names tls object No - TLS configuration

docs.title: Resource Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Default Description size string No micro Preset size cpu int No - CPU in millicores memory int No - Memory in MB

docs.title: Environment Variable Expansion
docs.text:
Environment variables support shell-style expansion:

environment:
  # Direct reference
  API_KEY: ${API_KEY}

  # With default value
  LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-info}

  # Literal dollar sign (escape with double)
  PRICE: $$100

docs.title: Duration Format
docs.text:
Duration fields accept Go-style duration strings:

  • 30s - 30 seconds
  • 5m - 5 minutes
  • 1h - 1 hour
  • 1h30m - 1 hour 30 minutes

docs.title: AWS LightSail
docs.text: Size vCPU Memory Est. Cost nano 0.25 512 MB ~$7/mo micro 0.5 1 GB ~$10/mo small 1 2 GB ~$25/mo medium 2 4 GB ~$50/mo large 4 8 GB ~$100/mo xlarge 8 16 GB ~$200/mo

docs.title: AWS ECS
docs.text:
Use cpu and memory fields directly:

resources:
  cpu: 256    # 0.25 vCPU
  memory: 512 # 512 MB

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
OmniDeploy validates configurations before deployment:

  • name must be alphanumeric with hyphens
  • container.image is required
  • At least one container.ports entry is required
  • container_port must be 1-65535
  • service.replicas must be non-negative

Use omnideploy preview to catch validation errors before deploying.

docs.title: Secrets Management
docs.text: Securely manage sensitive values in your deployments.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
OmniDeploy supports multiple approaches for handling secrets:

  1. Environment Variables - Pass at deploy time
  2. Pulumi Config - Encrypted in Pulumi state
  3. AWS Secrets Manager - Fetched at container runtime
  4. AWS SSM Parameter Store - Fetched at container runtime

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
The simplest approach - pass secrets when deploying:

# deploy.yaml
environment:
  API_KEY: ${API_KEY}
  DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL}

Deploy:

API_KEY=sk-... DATABASE_URL=postgres://... omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

Pros:

  • Simple, no setup required
  • Works everywhere

Cons:

  • Secrets visible in process list
  • Not suitable for shared CI/CD
  • No audit trail

docs.title: Pulumi Secrets
docs.text:
Store secrets encrypted in Pulumi state:

# Set secret
cd ~/.omnideploy/pulumi
pulumi stack select my-app
pulumi config set --secret api_key sk-...
pulumi config set --secret database_url postgres://...

Reference in deployment:

# deploy.yaml
environment:
  API_KEY: ${pulumi:api_key}
  DATABASE_URL: ${pulumi:database_url}

Pros:

  • Encrypted at rest
  • Version controlled (with state)
  • Team sharing via remote state

Cons:

  • Requires Pulumi CLI for management
  • Secrets in Pulumi state

docs.title: AWS Secrets Manager
docs.text: Store secrets in AWS and fetch at container runtime:

docs.title: 1. Create Secret
docs.text:
aws secretsmanager create-secret \
  --name my-app/api-key \
  --secret-string "sk-..."

aws secretsmanager create-secret \
  --name my-app/database-url \
  --secret-string "postgres://..."

docs.title: 2. Reference in Config
docs.text:
# deploy.yaml
secrets:
  - name: API_KEY
    source: secretsmanager:my-app/api-key

  - name: DATABASE_URL
    source: secretsmanager:my-app/database-url

docs.title: 3. Grant Access
docs.text:
Ensure your container has IAM permissions:

{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "secretsmanager:GetSecretValue"
      ],
      "Resource": [
        "arn:aws:secretsmanager:*:*:secret:my-app/*"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Pros:

  • Secrets never in config or state
  • Audit trail via CloudTrail
  • Automatic rotation support
  • Fine-grained access control

Cons:

  • AWS-specific
  • Additional cost (~$0.40/secret/month)
  • Requires IAM setup

docs.title: AWS SSM Parameter Store
docs.text: Similar to Secrets Manager, but simpler and cheaper:

docs.title: 1. Create Parameter
docs.text:
aws ssm put-parameter \
  --name /my-app/api-key \
  --value "sk-..." \
  --type SecureString

aws ssm put-parameter \
  --name /my-app/database-url \
  --value "postgres://..." \
  --type SecureString

docs.title: 2. Reference in Config
docs.text:
# deploy.yaml
secrets:
  - name: API_KEY
    source: ssm:/my-app/api-key

  - name: DATABASE_URL
    source: ssm:/my-app/database-url

Pros:

  • Free for standard parameters
  • Simple API
  • Integrated with AWS

Cons:

  • Less features than Secrets Manager
  • No automatic rotation

docs.title: Comparison
docs.text: Method Security Cost Complexity Audit Environment Variables Low Free Low None Pulumi Config Medium Free Medium Git history AWS Secrets Manager High ~$0.40/mo Medium CloudTrail AWS SSM High Free Medium CloudTrail

docs.title: 1. Never Commit Secrets
docs.text:
# .gitignore
.env
.env.*
*.pem
*.key
credentials.json

docs.title: 2. Use Different Secrets Per Environment
docs.text:
# Staging
aws secretsmanager create-secret --name staging/my-app/api-key ...

# Production
aws secretsmanager create-secret --name production/my-app/api-key ...

docs.title: 3. Rotate Secrets Regularly
docs.text: Set up automatic rotation in AWS Secrets Manager.

docs.title: 4. Audit Access
docs.text: Enable CloudTrail and monitor for unexpected secret access.

docs.title: 5. Least Privilege
docs.text:
Grant minimal IAM permissions:

{
  "Effect": "Allow",
  "Action": ["secretsmanager:GetSecretValue"],
  "Resource": ["arn:aws:secretsmanager:*:*:secret:my-app/*"],
  "Condition": {
    "StringEquals": {
      "aws:RequestTag/environment": "production"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text:
- name: Deploy
  env:
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
    # App secrets via AWS Secrets Manager - not in env
  run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

docs.title: With OIDC (Recommended)
docs.text:
- name: Configure AWS credentials
  uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
  with:
    role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/github-actions
    aws-region: us-east-1

- name: Deploy
  run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

docs.title: Continuous Deployment
docs.text: Strategies for automated deployments.

docs.title: 1. Trunk-Based Development
docs.text:
Deploy on every push to main:

# .github/workflows/deploy.yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest
      - run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

docs.title: 2. Release-Based
docs.text:
Deploy only on version tags:

on:
  push:
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production --yes

docs.title: 3. Environment Promotion
docs.text:
main → staging → production

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  deploy-staging:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    steps:
      - run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack staging --yes

  deploy-production:
    needs: deploy-staging
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production --yes

docs.title: Manual Rollback
docs.text:
# Deploy previous version
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production --yes
# (with previous image tag in config)

docs.title: Automated Rollback
docs.text:
deploy:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - name: Deploy
      id: deploy
      run: omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

    - name: Health check
      id: health
      run: curl -f https://my-app.example.com/health

    - name: Rollback on failure
      if: failure() && steps.deploy.outcome == 'success'
      run: |
        git checkout HEAD~1 -- deploy.yaml
        omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

docs.title: Blue-Green Deployments
docs.text:
Deploy new version alongside old, then switch:

# Deploy new version to blue stack
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack blue --yes

# Test blue
curl https://blue.example.com/health

# Switch traffic (DNS or load balancer)
# ...

# Destroy old green
omnideploy destroy --stack green --yes

docs.title: Canary Deployments
docs.text: Not directly supported by LightSail. Use ECS or Kubernetes targets for canary deployments with traffic splitting.

docs.title: Deployment Notifications
docs.text:
- name: Notify Slack
  if: always()
  uses: slackapi/slack-github-action@v1
  with:
    payload: |
      {
        "text": "Deployment ${{ job.status }}: ${{ github.repository }}"
      }
  env:
    SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK }}

docs.title: Health Checks
docs.text:
- name: Verify deployment
  run: |
    for i in {1..30}; do
      if curl -sf https://my-app.example.com/health; then
        echo "Deployment healthy"
        exit 0
      fi
      sleep 10
    done
    echo "Health check failed"
    exit 1

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Always preview first in CI before deploying
  2. Use environments for approval gates
  3. Monitor after deploy with health checks
  4. Have rollback plan ready
  5. Keep deployments small and frequent
  6. Use semantic versioning for releases

docs.title: GitHub Actions Deployment
docs.text: Automate deployments with GitHub Actions CI/CD.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Set up a complete CI/CD pipeline that:

  1. Builds your container image on push
  2. Pushes to GitHub Container Registry
  3. Deploys to your cloud provider

docs.title: Basic Workflow
docs.text:
Create .github/workflows/deploy.yaml:

name: Build and Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ['v*']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    outputs:
      image_tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=ref,event=pr
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=sha

      - name: Build and push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
    environment: production

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Go
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.24'

      - name: Install omnideploy
        run: go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest

      - name: Deploy
        env:
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          AWS_REGION: us-east-1
        run: |
          omnideploy up \
            --config deploy.yaml \
            --target lightsail \
            --backend pulumi \
            --stack production \
            --yes

docs.title: Environment-Based Deployments
docs.text:
Deploy to different environments based on branch:

name: Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'production' || 'staging' }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Go
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.24'

      - name: Install omnideploy
        run: go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest

      - name: Set environment
        id: env
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.ref }}" == "refs/heads/main" ]; then
            echo "stack=production" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "config=deploy.prod.yaml" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "stack=staging" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "config=deploy.staging.yaml" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Deploy
        env:
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          AWS_REGION: us-east-1
        run: |
          omnideploy up \
            --config ${{ steps.env.outputs.config }} \
            --stack ${{ steps.env.outputs.stack }} \
            --yes

docs.title: Tag-Based Releases
docs.text:
Deploy only on version tags:

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Get version
        id: version
        run: echo "version=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Build and push image
        # ... build steps ...

      - name: Update deploy config
        run: |
          sed -i "s|image:.*|image: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}|" deploy.yaml

      - name: Deploy
        env:
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
        run: |
          omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production --yes

docs.title: Preview on Pull Requests
docs.text:
Show deployment preview on PRs:

name: Preview

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  preview:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Go
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.24'

      - name: Install omnideploy
        run: go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest

      - name: Preview deployment
        id: preview
        env:
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
        run: |
          OUTPUT=$(omnideploy preview --config deploy.yaml 2>&1)
          echo "preview<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "$OUTPUT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Comment on PR
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: `## Deployment Preview\n\n\`\`\`\n${{ steps.preview.outputs.preview }}\n\`\`\``
            })

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
Full workflow for an OmniAgent application:

name: CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ['v*']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.24'
      - run: go test ./...

  build:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        id: meta
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' }}
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  deploy-staging:
    needs: build
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.24'
      - run: go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest
      - name: Deploy to staging
        env:
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          omnideploy up \
            --config deploy.yaml \
            --stack staging \
            --yes

  deploy-production:
    needs: [build, deploy-staging]
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: '1.24'
      - run: go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest
      - name: Deploy to production
        env:
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          omnideploy up \
            --config deploy.yaml \
            --stack production \
            --yes

docs.title: GitLab CI Deployment
docs.text: Deploy with GitLab CI/CD pipelines.

docs.title: Basic Pipeline
docs.text:
Create .gitlab-ci.yml:

stages:
  - build
  - deploy

variables:
  REGISTRY: registry.gitlab.com
  IMAGE_NAME: $CI_PROJECT_PATH

build:
  stage: build
  image: docker:24
  services:
    - docker:24-dind
  script:
    - docker login -u $CI_REGISTRY_USER -p $CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD $CI_REGISTRY
    - docker build -t $REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:$CI_COMMIT_SHA .
    - docker push $REGISTRY/$IMAGE_NAME:$CI_COMMIT_SHA
  only:
    - main
    - tags

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  image: golang:1.24
  before_script:
    - go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest
  script:
    - omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack $CI_ENVIRONMENT_SLUG --yes
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://my-app.example.com
  only:
    - main

docs.title: Local Deployment
docs.text: Deploy to cloud infrastructure from your local machine.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. OmniDeploy installed
  2. Cloud credentials configured
  3. Container image built and pushed to a registry

docs.title: Step 1: Build Container Image
docs.text:
# Build image
docker build -t my-app:latest .

# Tag for registry
docker tag my-app:latest ghcr.io/myorg/my-app:latest

# Or with version
docker tag my-app:latest ghcr.io/myorg/my-app:v1.0.0

docs.title: Step 2: Push to Registry
docs.text:
GitHub Container RegistryAmazon ECRDocker Hub 
# Login to GHCR
echo $GITHUB_TOKEN | docker login ghcr.io -u $GITHUB_USER --password-stdin

# Push image
docker push ghcr.io/myorg/my-app:latest

# Login to ECR
aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 | \
  docker login --username AWS --password-stdin 123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com

# Create repository (first time)
aws ecr create-repository --repository-name my-app

# Tag and push
docker tag my-app:latest 123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/my-app:latest
docker push 123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/my-app:latest

# Login
docker login -u $DOCKER_USER

# Push
docker push myorg/my-app:latest

docs.title: Step 3: Configure Deployment
docs.text:
Create deploy.yaml:

name: my-app
region: us-east-1

container:
  image: ghcr.io/myorg/my-app:latest
  ports:
    - container_port: 8080
      protocol: HTTP
  health_check:
    path: /health
    interval: 30s

service:
  replicas: 1
  public: true

resources:
  size: small

environment:
  LOG_LEVEL: info

docs.title: Step 4: Set Cloud Credentials
docs.text:
AWSDigitalOcean 
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA...
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
export AWS_REGION=us-east-1

export DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN=...

docs.title: Step 5: Preview Changes
docs.text:
Always preview before deploying:

omnideploy preview --config deploy.yaml

Output:

Previewing deployment to lightsail using pulumi...

Changes:
  + aws:lightsail:ContainerService my-app
  + aws:lightsail:ContainerServiceDeploymentVersion my-app-deployment

Changes: 2 create, 0 update, 0 delete

docs.title: Step 6: Deploy
docs.text:
# Deploy with confirmation prompt
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

# Deploy with auto-approve
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

# Deploy to named stack
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production

docs.title: Step 7: Verify Deployment
docs.text:
The output includes the service URL:

Outputs:
    service_name: "my-app"
    state       : "RUNNING"
    url         : "https://my-app.abc123.us-east-1.cs.amazonlightsail.com"

Test the endpoint:

curl https://my-app.abc123.us-east-1.cs.amazonlightsail.com/health

docs.title: Updating Deployments
docs.text:
To update a running deployment:

  1. Build and push a new image: 

    docker build -t ghcr.io/myorg/my-app:v1.1.0 .
docker push ghcr.io/myorg/my-app:v1.1.0

  2. Update deploy.yaml with new image tag: 

    container:
  image: ghcr.io/myorg/my-app:v1.1.0

  3. Deploy the update: 

    omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production

docs.title: Option 1: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Pass secrets via environment when deploying:

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

Reference in config:

environment:
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}

docs.title: Option 2: Pulumi Config
docs.text:
Store secrets in Pulumi config:

cd ~/.omnideploy/pulumi
pulumi config set --secret api_key sk-...

docs.title: Option 3: AWS Secrets Manager
docs.text:
Reference secrets from AWS:

secrets:
  - name: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
    source: secretsmanager:my-app/anthropic-api-key

docs.title: Multi-Environment Deployments
docs.text: Create separate configs or use stacks:

docs.title: Option A: Separate Config Files
docs.text:
# deploy.staging.yaml
omnideploy up --config deploy.staging.yaml --stack staging

# deploy.prod.yaml
omnideploy up --config deploy.prod.yaml --stack production

docs.title: Option B: Same Config, Different Stacks
docs.text:
# Staging (smaller resources)
REPLICAS=1 SIZE=micro omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack staging

# Production (larger resources)
REPLICAS=3 SIZE=medium omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production

docs.title: View Pulumi State
docs.text:
cd ~/.omnideploy/pulumi
pulumi stack ls
pulumi stack select my-app
pulumi stack

docs.title: View Deployment Logs
docs.text:
For LightSail:

aws lightsail get-container-log \
  --service-name my-app \
  --container-name my-app

docs.title: Force Refresh State
docs.text: omnideploy refresh --stack my-app

docs.title: Destroy and Recreate
docs.text:
omnideploy destroy --stack my-app --yes
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack my-app

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• GitHub Actions - Automate deployments with CI/CD
  • Continuous Deployment - Set up automatic deployments

docs.title: AWS Setup
docs.text: Configure AWS credentials and permissions for OmniDeploy.

docs.title: Quick Start with Bootstrap Command
docs.text:
The easiest way to set up AWS IAM resources is using the omnideploy bootstrap command:

# Using admin credentials, create policy and group
omnideploy bootstrap run

# Or create a user with access keys
omnideploy bootstrap run --user deployer --create-key

This creates:

  • OmniDeployPolicy - IAM policy with required permissions
  • omnideploy-users - IAM group with the policy attached
  • Optionally, an IAM user with access keys

See Bootstrap Command for details.

docs.title: Supported Regions
docs.text:
LightSail Container Services are available in these regions:

Region Location us-west-2 Oregon (default) us-east-1 N. Virginia us-east-2 Ohio eu-west-1 Ireland eu-west-2 London eu-west-3 Paris eu-central-1 Frankfurt ap-south-1 Mumbai ap-northeast-1 Tokyo ap-northeast-2 Seoul ap-southeast-1 Singapore ap-southeast-2 Sydney ca-central-1 Canada

Note: us-west-1 (N. California) does NOT support LightSail containers.

docs.title: Required Permissions
docs.text: OmniDeploy needs permissions for the deployment target and container registry.

docs.title: LightSail Target
docs.text: Permission Purpose lightsail:CreateContainerService Create container service lightsail:CreateContainerServiceDeployment Deploy containers lightsail:GetContainerServices Check service status lightsail:DeleteContainerService Destroy resources lightsail:UpdateContainerService Update configuration

docs.title: ECR (Container Registry)
docs.text: Permission Purpose ecr:GetAuthorizationToken Authenticate Docker ecr:CreateRepository Create image repository ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability Push images ecr:PutImage Push images ecr:BatchGetImage Pull images

docs.title: SSM (Secrets)
docs.text: Permission Purpose ssm:GetParameter Read secrets ssm:PutParameter Store secrets (optional)

docs.title: Option 1: Managed Policies (Recommended)
docs.text:
Attach AWS-managed policies for simplicity:

  1. Go to IAM Console → Users → Create user

  2. Enter username (e.g., omnideploy)

  3. Select Attach policies directly

  4. Search and attach these managed policies:

Policy Permissions AmazonLightsailFullAccess Full LightSail access AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryFullAccess Full ECR access AmazonSSMReadOnlyAccess Read SSM parameters

  1. Click Create user

  2. Go to Security credentials → Create access key

  3. Select Command Line Interface (CLI)

  4. Download or copy the access key and secret

docs.title: Option 2: Custom Policy (Least Privilege)
docs.text:
For production, create a custom policy with minimal permissions:

  1. Go to IAM Console → Policies → Create policy

  2. Use this JSON:

{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "LightSailContainers",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "lightsail:CreateContainerService",
        "lightsail:CreateContainerServiceDeployment",
        "lightsail:CreateContainerServiceRegistryLogin",
        "lightsail:DeleteContainerService",
        "lightsail:GetContainerServiceDeployments",
        "lightsail:GetContainerServices",
        "lightsail:GetContainerLog",
        "lightsail:RegisterContainerImage",
        "lightsail:UpdateContainerService"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Sid": "ECR",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "ecr:GetAuthorizationToken",
        "ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability",
        "ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer",
        "ecr:BatchGetImage",
        "ecr:PutImage",
        "ecr:InitiateLayerUpload",
        "ecr:UploadLayerPart",
        "ecr:CompleteLayerUpload",
        "ecr:CreateRepository",
        "ecr:DescribeRepositories"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Sid": "SSMSecrets",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "ssm:GetParameter",
        "ssm:GetParameters"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:ssm:*:*:parameter/*"
    }
  ]
}

  1. Name it OmniDeployPolicy

  2. Create IAM user and attach this policy

docs.title: Option 3: IAM Group (Teams)
docs.text:
For teams, use groups:

  1. Create IAM group omnideploy-users
  2. Attach policies to the group
  3. Add users to the group

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="AKIA..."
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="..."
export AWS_REGION="us-west-2"

docs.title: AWS CLI Profile
docs.text:
aws configure --profile omnideploy
# Enter access key, secret, region

export AWS_PROFILE="omnideploy"

docs.title: Shared Credentials File
docs.text:
Add to ~/.aws/credentials:

[omnideploy]
aws_access_key_id = AKIA...
aws_secret_access_key = ...

Add to ~/.aws/config:

[profile omnideploy]
region = us-west-2
output = json

docs.title: Verify Setup
docs.text:
# Check identity
aws sts get-caller-identity

# Expected output:
{
    "UserId": "AIDA...",
    "Account": "123456789012",
    "Arn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/omnideploy"
}

docs.title: Setting Up ECR
docs.text:
Create a repository for your container images:

# Create repository
aws ecr create-repository \
    --repository-name my-app \
    --region us-west-2

# Get repository URI
aws ecr describe-repositories \
    --repository-names my-app \
    --query 'repositories[0].repositoryUri' \
    --output text
# Output: 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/my-app

docs.title: Push Image to ECR
docs.text:
# Login to ECR
aws ecr get-login-password --region us-west-2 | \
    docker login --username AWS --password-stdin \
    123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com

# Tag image
docker tag my-app:latest \
    123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/my-app:latest

# Push
docker push 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/my-app:latest

docs.title: Update deploy.yaml
docs.text:
container:
  image: 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/my-app:latest

docs.title: Using GHCR with Private Repos
docs.text:
If using GitHub Container Registry with private images, configure registry credentials:

container:
  image: ghcr.io/owner/repo:latest
  registry:
    server: ghcr.io
    username: github-username
    password_env: GITHUB_TOKEN

Create a GitHub token with read:packages scope and set:

export GITHUB_TOKEN="ghp_..."

docs.title: IAM Roles for CI/CD
docs.text:
For GitHub Actions, use OIDC instead of access keys:

  1. Create IAM Identity Provider for GitHub
  2. Create IAM Role with trust policy for GitHub
  3. Attach OmniDeploy permissions to the role

See GitHub Actions deployment for details.

docs.title: "Access Denied" Errors
docs.text:
Check that your IAM user has the required policies attached:

aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name omnideploy

docs.title: "Token Expired" Errors
docs.text:
Refresh credentials:

# For SSO
aws sso login --profile your-profile

# For access keys, create new ones in IAM Console

docs.title: ECR Login Fails
docs.text:
Ensure you have ecr:GetAuthorizationToken permission:

aws ecr get-login-password --region us-west-2

If this fails, check IAM permissions.

docs.title: Bootstrap Command
docs.text: The omnideploy bootstrap command automates IAM setup.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
# Create policy and group (requires admin credentials)
omnideploy bootstrap run

# Create policy, group, and user with access keys
omnideploy bootstrap run --user deployer --create-key

# Check current status
omnideploy bootstrap status

# View the IAM policy document
omnideploy bootstrap policy

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Get admin credentials - Use AWS Console or existing admin profile

  2. Run bootstrap: 

    export AWS_PROFILE=admin  # or export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/SECRET
omnideploy bootstrap run --user deployer --create-key

  3. Save the output - Copy the access key and secret

  4. Use new credentials for deployments: 

    export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID="AKIA..."
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY="..."
export AWS_REGION="us-west-2"

omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: What Bootstrap Creates
docs.text: Resource Name Description IAM Policy OmniDeployPolicy Permissions for LightSail, ECR, SSM IAM Group omnideploy-users Group with policy attached IAM User (optional) User in the group Access Key (optional) Credentials for the user

docs.title: Idempotent
docs.text: The bootstrap command is safe to run multiple times. It checks for existing resources and skips creation if they already exist.

docs.title: Concepts
docs.text: Understanding how OmniDeploy works.

docs.title: The Three Pillars
docs.text: OmniDeploy separates deployment into three independent concerns:

docs.title: 1. Targets (WHERE to deploy)
docs.text:
A target defines the cloud platform and service where your container runs.

Target Description Best For lightsail AWS LightSail Container Service Simple apps, cost-effective ecs AWS ECS with Fargate Production workloads, auto-scaling agentcore AWS Bedrock AgentCore AI agent deployments kubernetes Any Kubernetes cluster Complex orchestration digitalocean DigitalOcean App Platform Simple PaaS experience

Each target understands how to translate your deployment config into the specific resources needed.

docs.title: 2. Backends (HOW to provision)
docs.text:
A backend is the Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tool that creates and manages cloud resources.

Backend Description State Storage pulumi Pulumi Automation API Local or cloud cdk AWS Cloud Development Kit CloudFormation terraform Terraform HCL generation Local or remote

Backends are interchangeable. You can switch backends without changing your config.

docs.title: 3. Runtime Adapters (WHAT to deploy)
docs.text:
A runtime adapter converts application-specific configuration to the universal deployment format.

Adapter Detects Extracts omniagent OmniAgent configs Gateway port, agent settings agentkit AgentKit configs Agent runtime settings container Generic deploy configs Direct pass-through

docs.title: DeployConfig Schema
docs.text:
The universal deployment configuration that all targets understand:

# Identity
name: my-app              # Required: deployment name
version: "1.0.0"          # Optional: version tag
region: us-east-1         # Cloud region

# Container settings
container:
  image: nginx:latest     # Container image
  command: []             # Entrypoint override
  args: []                # Command arguments
  ports:                  # Port mappings
    - container_port: 80
      protocol: HTTP
  health_check:           # Health check config
    path: /health
    interval: 30s
    timeout: 5s

# Service settings
service:
  replicas: 1             # Instance count
  public: true            # Public access
  domains: []             # Custom domains

# Resource allocation
resources:
  size: micro             # Preset size

# Environment
environment:              # Key-value pairs
  LOG_LEVEL: info

# Tags
tags:                     # Resource tags
  team: platform

docs.title: Stacks
docs.text:
A stack is an isolated deployment instance. You can have multiple stacks from the same config:

# Deploy to staging
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack staging

# Deploy to production
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production

Each stack maintains its own:

  • Cloud resources
  • State (tracked by the backend)
  • Outputs (URLs, IPs, etc.)

docs.title: State Management
docs.text:
OmniDeploy uses the backend's state management:

Backend Default State Location Remote Options Pulumi ~/.omnideploy/pulumi/ S3, Pulumi Cloud CDK CloudFormation N/A (always in AWS) Terraform ~/.omnideploy/terraform/ S3, Terraform Cloud

docs.title: Resource Lifecycle
docs.text:
preview → up → (running) → destroy
   │       │                  │
   │       │                  └── Removes all resources
   │       └── Creates/updates resources
   └── Shows what would change (dry run)

docs.title: 1. Use Named Stacks
docs.text:
Always use explicit stack names for clarity:

omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack my-app-prod

docs.title: 2. Preview Before Apply
docs.text:
Always preview changes before deploying:

omnideploy preview --config deploy.yaml

docs.title: 3. Use Environment Variables for Secrets
docs.text:
Don't put secrets in config files:

environment:
  API_KEY: ${API_KEY}  # Resolved from environment

docs.title: 4. Tag Resources
docs.text:
Add tags for cost tracking and organization:

tags:
  environment: production
  team: platform
  cost-center: engineering

docs.title: 5. Use Health Checks
docs.text:
Always configure health checks for reliable deployments:

container:
  health_check:
    path: /health
    interval: 30s
    timeout: 5s
    healthy_threshold: 2
    unhealthy_threshold: 3

docs.title: Why Two Libraries?
docs.text: Command Library Reason bootstrap AWS SDK v2 One-time IAM setup, no state needed up/preview/destroy Pulumi IaC with state management, drift detection, rollback

docs.title: Bootstrap (AWS SDK v2)
docs.text:
The bootstrap command uses AWS SDK v2 directly because:

  • Simple operations - Just create IAM policy, group, user
  • No state needed - Idempotent, can run multiple times
  • No Pulumi dependency - Works without Pulumi installed

docs.title: Deployments (Pulumi Automation API)
docs.text:
Deployment commands use Pulumi because:

  • State management - Tracks what resources exist
  • Dependency ordering - Creates resources in correct order
  • Drift detection - Knows if resources changed externally
  • Updates - Can modify existing resources safely
  • Rollback - Can undo failed deployments

docs.title: Credentials Flow
docs.text:
Both libraries read AWS credentials from:

  1. Environment variables (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)
  2. Shared credentials file (~/.aws/credentials)
  3. AWS config profiles (AWS_PROFILE)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Configuration Schema - Full configuration reference
  • Targets - Explore deployment targets
  • Backends - Learn about IaC backends

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.24+ (for installation from source)
  • AWS credentials (for AWS targets)
  • Pulumi CLI (optional, for advanced Pulumi operations)

docs.title: Install from Source
docs.text:
go install github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/cmd/omnideploy@latest

Verify installation:

omnideploy --help

docs.title: Install from Binary
docs.text:
Download the latest release from GitHub Releases:

macOS (Apple Silicon)macOS (Intel)Linux (x86_64) 
curl -L https://github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/releases/latest/download/omnideploy-darwin-arm64.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv omnideploy /usr/local/bin/

curl -L https://github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/releases/latest/download/omnideploy-darwin-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv omnideploy /usr/local/bin/

curl -L https://github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/releases/latest/download/omnideploy-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv omnideploy /usr/local/bin/

docs.title: AWS Credentials
docs.text: OmniDeploy needs AWS credentials for AWS targets (LightSail, ECS, AgentCore).

docs.title: Option 1: Environment Variables
docs.text:
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIA...
export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
export AWS_REGION=us-east-1

docs.title: Option 2: AWS CLI Profile
docs.text:
aws configure
# or for a named profile
aws configure --profile omnideploy
export AWS_PROFILE=omnideploy

docs.title: Option 3: IAM Role (EC2/ECS)
docs.text: If running on AWS infrastructure, use an IAM instance role or task role.

docs.title: Pulumi Setup (Optional)
docs.text:
For the Pulumi backend, you can optionally install the Pulumi CLI:

# macOS
brew install pulumi

# Linux
curl -fsSL https://get.pulumi.com | sh

OmniDeploy uses Pulumi's Automation API and doesn't require the CLI for basic operations, but the CLI is useful for:

  • Viewing stack state: pulumi stack
  • Manual state management: pulumi state
  • Stack export/import

docs.title: Pulumi State Storage
docs.text:
By default, OmniDeploy stores Pulumi state locally at ~/.omnideploy/pulumi/.

For team collaboration, configure a remote backend:

# AWS S3
export PULUMI_BACKEND_URL=s3://my-bucket/pulumi-state

# Pulumi Cloud
pulumi login

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
Check that everything is working:

# Show version
omnideploy version

# List available targets
omnideploy targets

# List available backends
omnideploy backends

# List runtime adapters
omnideploy runtimes

Expected output:

Available targets:
  lightsail    AWS LightSail Container Service - simple, cost-effective container hosting

Available backends:
  pulumi       Pulumi - Infrastructure as Code using Go

Available runtime adapters:
  omniagent    OmniAgent gateway with web UI
  container    Generic container deployment

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Start - Deploy your first application
  • Concepts - Understand how OmniDeploy works

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Deploy your first container application to AWS LightSail in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• OmniDeploy installed
  • AWS credentials configured
  • A container image (we'll use nginx for this example)

docs.title: Step 1: Create Configuration
docs.text:
Create a deploy.yaml file:

name: hello-world
region: us-east-1

container:
  image: nginx:latest
  ports:
    - container_port: 80
      protocol: HTTP

service:
  replicas: 1
  public: true

resources:
  size: micro  # ~$10/month

docs.title: Step 2: Preview Deployment
docs.text:
See what resources will be created:

omnideploy preview --config deploy.yaml

Output:

Previewing deployment to lightsail using pulumi...

Changes:
  + aws:lightsail:ContainerService hello-world
  + aws:lightsail:ContainerServiceDeploymentVersion hello-world-deployment

Changes: 2 create, 0 update, 0 delete

docs.title: Step 3: Deploy
docs.text:
Deploy to AWS LightSail:

omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

Output:

Deploying to lightsail using pulumi...

Updating (hello-world):
     Type                                              Name                    Status
 +   aws:lightsail:ContainerService                   hello-world             created
 +   aws:lightsail:ContainerServiceDeploymentVersion  hello-world-deployment  created

Outputs:
    service_name: "hello-world"
    state       : "RUNNING"
    url         : "https://hello-world.abc123.us-east-1.cs.amazonlightsail.com"

Resources:
    + 2 created

Deployment complete!

docs.title: Step 4: Access Your App
docs.text: Open the URL from the output in your browser. You should see the nginx welcome page.

docs.title: Step 5: Clean Up
docs.text:
When you're done, destroy the resources:

omnideploy destroy --stack hello-world --yes

docs.title: Option A: Generic Container
docs.text:
Update the image to your own:

name: my-api
region: us-east-1

container:
  image: ghcr.io/myorg/my-api:latest
  ports:
    - container_port: 8080
      protocol: HTTP
  health_check:
    path: /health
    interval: 30s
    timeout: 5s

service:
  replicas: 2
  public: true

resources:
  size: small

environment:
  LOG_LEVEL: info
  DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL}

docs.title: Option B: OmniAgent Application
docs.text:
If you have an OmniAgent-based app, omnideploy auto-detects the config:

# omniagent.yaml
gateway:
  address: "0.0.0.0:18789"

agent:
  provider: anthropic
  model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  api_key: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}

# Deployment settings
deploy:
  name: my-agent
  region: us-east-1
  image: ghcr.io/myorg/my-agent:latest
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    size: small

Deploy with:

omnideploy up --config omniagent.yaml --runtime omniagent

docs.title: Common Operations
docs.text:
# Preview changes
omnideploy preview --config deploy.yaml

# Deploy with auto-approve
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --yes

# Deploy to a named stack
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --stack production

# Use a different target
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --target ecs

# Use a different backend
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --backend terraform

# Destroy a stack
omnideploy destroy --stack my-app --yes

docs.title: Go API Reference
docs.text: Use OmniDeploy programmatically in Go applications.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: go get github.com/plexusone/omnideploy

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "log"

    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/backend"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/backend/pulumi"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/config"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/deploy"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/target/lightsail"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Create deployer
    deployer := deploy.New(
        deploy.WithTarget(&lightsail.Target{}),
        deploy.WithBackend(pulumi.New()),
    )

    // Deploy from config file
    result, err := deployer.Apply(ctx, "deploy.yaml", backend.ApplyOptions{
        StackName:   "my-app",
        AutoApprove: true,
    })
    if err != nil {
        log.Fatal(err)
    }

    fmt.Printf("Deployed: %s\n", result.Outputs["url"])
}

docs.title: Extending OmniDeploy
docs.text: Add custom targets, backends, and runtime adapters.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
OmniDeploy uses a pluggable architecture with three extension points:

  1. Targets - Where to deploy
  2. Backends - How to provision
  3. Runtime Adapters - What to deploy

All extensions use Go's init() function for auto-registration.

docs.title: Adding a Target
docs.text: Create a new target to support a different cloud platform.

docs.title: Interface
docs.text:
// target/target.go
type Target interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Validate(cfg *config.DeployConfig) error
    ResourceSpec(cfg *config.DeployConfig) (*ResourceSpec, error)
}

docs.title: Example: Custom Target
docs.text:
// target/mytarget/mytarget.go
package mytarget

import (
    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/config"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/target"
)

func init() {
    target.Register(&Target{})
}

type Target struct{}

func (t *Target) Name() string {
    return "mytarget"
}

func (t *Target) Description() string {
    return "My custom deployment target"
}

func (t *Target) Validate(cfg *config.DeployConfig) error {
    if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    // Target-specific validation
    return nil
}

func (t *Target) ResourceSpec(cfg *config.DeployConfig) (*target.ResourceSpec, error) {
    if err := t.Validate(cfg); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }

    resources := []target.Resource{
        {
            Type: "my_resource_type",
            Name: cfg.Name,
            Properties: map[string]any{
                "image":    cfg.Container.Image,
                "replicas": cfg.Service.Replicas,
            },
        },
    }

    return &target.ResourceSpec{
        StackName: cfg.Name,
        Region:    cfg.Region,
        Target:    t.Name(),
        Config:    cfg,
        Resources: resources,
        Outputs: []target.Output{
            {Name: "url", Description: "Service URL"},
        },
    }, nil
}

    _ "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/target/mytarget"

docs.title: Adding a Runtime Adapter
docs.text: Create a new adapter to support a different application format.

docs.title: Interface
docs.text:
// runtime/adapter.go
type Adapter interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Detect(path string) bool
    Load(path string) (*config.DeployConfig, error)
}

docs.title: Example: Custom Adapter
docs.text:
// runtime/myapp/adapter.go
package myapp

import (
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "strings"

    "gopkg.in/yaml.v3"

    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/config"
    "github.com/plexusone/omnideploy/runtime"
)

func init() {
    runtime.Register(&Adapter{})
}

type Adapter struct{}

func (a *Adapter) Name() string {
    return "myapp"
}

func (a *Adapter) Description() string {
    return "MyApp framework configuration"
}

func (a *Adapter) Detect(path string) bool {
    base := strings.ToLower(filepath.Base(path))
    return strings.Contains(base, "myapp")
}

func (a *Adapter) Load(path string) (*config.DeployConfig, error) {
    data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }

    var myConfig MyAppConfig
    if err := yaml.Unmarshal(data, &myConfig); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }

    // Convert to DeployConfig
    deployConfig := &config.DeployConfig{
        Name:   myConfig.AppName,
        Region: myConfig.Deploy.Region,
        Container: config.ContainerConfig{
            Image: myConfig.Deploy.Image,
            Ports: []config.PortMapping{
                {ContainerPort: myConfig.Port, Protocol: "HTTP"},
            },
        },
        Service: config.ServiceConfig{
            Replicas: myConfig.Deploy.Replicas,
            Public:   true,
        },
        Resources: config.ResourceConfig{
            Size: myConfig.Deploy.Size,
        },
        Environment: myConfig.Env,
    }

    deployConfig.Defaults()
    return deployConfig, nil
}

type MyAppConfig struct {
    AppName string            `yaml:"app_name"`
    Port    int               `yaml:"port"`
    Env     map[string]string `yaml:"env"`
    Deploy  struct {
        Image    string `yaml:"image"`
        Region   string `yaml:"region"`
        Replicas int    `yaml:"replicas"`
        Size     string `yaml:"size"`
    } `yaml:"deploy"`
}

docs.text:
func TestMyTarget_Validate(t *testing.T) {
    target := &mytarget.Target{}

    cfg := &config.DeployConfig{
        Name: "test",
        Container: config.ContainerConfig{
            Image: "nginx:latest",
            Ports: []config.PortMapping{{ContainerPort: 80}},
        },
    }

    err := target.Validate(cfg)

docs.title: Integration Tests
docs.text:
func TestMyTarget_Deploy(t *testing.T) {
    if testing.Short() {
        t.Skip("skipping integration test")
    }

    ctx := context.Background()
    deployer := deploy.New(
        deploy.WithTarget(&mytarget.Target{}),
        deploy.WithBackend(pulumi.New()),
    )

    cfg := &config.DeployConfig{...}
    result, err := deployer.DeployFromConfig(ctx, cfg, backend.ApplyOptions{})
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatal(err)
    }

    // Verify deployment
    if result.Outputs["url"] == "" {
        t.Error("expected URL output")
    }

    // Cleanup
    deployer.Destroy(ctx, cfg.Name, backend.DestroyOptions{})
}

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Auto-register with init(): Use init() for automatic registration
  2. Validate early: Validate configuration before provisioning
  3. Handle errors gracefully: Return meaningful error messages
  4. Support preview: Implement Preview() for dry-run support
  5. Document requirements: Document any external dependencies
  6. Test thoroughly: Include unit and integration tests

The AgentKit adapter understands AgentKit agent configurations:

docs.title: Container Runtime Adapter
docs.text: Generic container deployment using the OmniDeploy config format.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Container adapter is a pass-through that reads OmniDeploy's native configuration format directly. Use this for any containerized application.

docs.title: Detection
docs.text:
Files are detected as container configs when:

  1. Filename contains "deploy" or "omnideploy"
  2. No application-specific patterns detected

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
name: my-api
version: "1.0.0"
region: us-east-1

container:
  image: nginx:latest
  command: []
  args: []
  ports:
    - container_port: 80
      protocol: HTTP
      name: http
  health_check:
    path: /
    interval: 30s
    timeout: 5s
    healthy_threshold: 2
    unhealthy_threshold: 3

service:
  replicas: 1
  public: true

resources:
  size: micro

environment:
  LOG_LEVEL: info
  DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL}

tags:
  environment: production
  team: platform

docs.title: Validations
docs.text:
The container adapter validates:

  • name is required
  • container.image is required
  • At least one port is required
  • Port numbers are 1-65535
  • Protocol is HTTP, HTTPS, TCP, or UDP

docs.title: OmniAgent Runtime Adapter
docs.text: Deploy OmniAgent gateway applications.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The OmniAgent adapter understands OmniAgent configuration files and extracts:

  • Gateway port configuration
  • Agent provider and model settings
  • Deployment parameters

docs.title: Detection
docs.text:
Files are detected as OmniAgent configs when:

  1. Filename contains "omniagent"
  2. File contains gateway: or agent: top-level keys

docs.title: OmniAgent Config with Deploy Section
docs.text:
# omniagent.yaml

# OmniAgent configuration
gateway:
  address: "0.0.0.0:18789"
  api_keys:
    - ${GATEWAY_API_KEY}

agent:
  provider: anthropic
  model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  api_key: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}

# Deployment configuration (omnideploy-specific)
deploy:
  name: my-omniagent
  region: us-east-1
  image: ghcr.io/myorg/my-omniagent:latest
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    size: small
  environment:
    LOG_LEVEL: info

docs.title: What Gets Extracted
docs.text: OmniAgent Config DeployConfig gateway.address port container.ports[0].container_port deploy.name name deploy.image container.image deploy.region region deploy.replicas service.replicas deploy.resources.size resources.size deploy.environment environment agent.provider environment.OMNIAGENT_AGENT_PROVIDER agent.model environment.OMNIAGENT_AGENT_MODEL

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
# Auto-detect adapter
omnideploy up --config omniagent.yaml

# Explicit adapter
omnideploy up --config my-config.yaml --runtime omniagent

docs.title: Defaults
docs.text:
If not specified:

Field Default deploy.name Filename without extension deploy.image ghcr.io/plexusone/omniagent:latest deploy.region us-east-1 deploy.replicas 1 deploy.resources.size micro Container port 18789 (OmniAgent default)

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text:
Auto-configured to:

health_check:
  path: /api/health
  port: 18789
  interval: 30s
  timeout: 5s
  healthy_threshold: 2
  unhealthy_threshold: 3

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
The adapter automatically sets:

environment:
  OMNIAGENT_AGENT_PROVIDER: anthropic  # from agent.provider
  OMNIAGENT_AGENT_MODEL: claude-...    # from agent.model

docs.title: Secrets Handling
docs.text:
API keys referenced with ${VAR} are converted to secrets:

# In omniagent.yaml
agent:
  api_key: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}

# Results in DeployConfig
environment:
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}  # Resolved at deploy time

Pass secrets when deploying:

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... omnideploy up --config omniagent.yaml

    size: small
  environment:

    TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID: ${TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID}
    TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN: ${TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN}

Deploy:

export TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=AC...

omnideploy up --config omniagent.yaml --stack production

docs.text: Feature LightSail ECS Kubernetes Auto-scaling ❌ ✓ ✓ Load balancing ✓ (built-in) ✓ (ALB/NLB) ✓ (Ingress) Custom domains ✓ ✓ ✓ SSL/TLS ✓ (auto) ✓ (ACM) ✓ (cert-manager) Persistent storage ❌ ✓ (EFS) ✓ (PVC) GPU support ❌ ✓ ✓ Complexity Low Medium High

docs.title: Decision Guide
docs.text:
Start here:
    │
    ├─► Need simple, predictable pricing?
    │       └─► LightSail
    │
    ├─► Need auto-scaling for variable traffic?
    │       └─► ECS Fargate
    │
    ├─► Deploying AI agents to AWS Bedrock?
    │       └─► AgentCore
    │
    ├─► Have existing Kubernetes cluster?
    │       └─► Kubernetes
    │
    └─► Want simple PaaS outside AWS?
            └─► DigitalOcean

docs.title: Using Targets
docs.text:
Specify target with --target flag:

# LightSail (default)
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --target lightsail

# ECS
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --target ecs

# Kubernetes
omnideploy up --config deploy.yaml --target kubernetes

docs.title: Target-Specific Configuration
docs.text:
Some config fields are target-specific:

# LightSail-specific
resources:
  size: micro  # nano, micro, small, medium, large, xlarge

# ECS-specific
resources:
  cpu: 256     # Fargate CPU units
  memory: 512  # Fargate memory MB

# Kubernetes-specific
resources:
  cpu: 250m    # Kubernetes CPU request
  memory: 256Mi # Kubernetes memory request

docs.title: List Available Targets
docs.text:
omnideploy targets

Output:

Available targets:
  lightsail    AWS LightSail Container Service - simple, cost-effective container hosting
